content:
# John Chapter 15 — The EMET Bible (Literal)
**Translator:** EMET | **Source:** Codex Sinaiticus (א), 4th century CE | **Source Image:** source-texts/greek-NT/JHN_015_page_001.jpg

1. "I am the true vine, and the Father of me is the vinedresser.

2. Every branch in me not bearing fruit, he takes away; and every [one] bearing fruit, he prunes it, that it may bear more fruit.

3. Already you are clean because of the word that I have spoken to you.

4. Abide in me, and I in you. Just as the branch is not able to bear fruit from itself unless it abides in the vine, thus neither [can] you unless in me you abide.

5. I am the vine, you [are] the branches. The [one] abiding in me and I in him, this [one] bears much fruit, because apart from me you are not able to do nothing.

6. If anyone does not abide in me, he is cast out as the branch and is withered, and they gather them and into the fire they throw [them], and they are burned.

7. If you abide in me and the words of me abide in you, ask whatever you wish, and it will happen to you.

8. In this the Father of me is glorified, that you bear much fruit and become disciples of me.

9. Just as the Father loved me, I also loved you; abide in the love of me.

10. If you keep the commandments of me, you will abide in the love of me, just as I have kept the commandments of the Father of me and abide in the love of him.

11. These things I have spoken to you that the joy of me may be in you and the joy of you may be made full.

12. This is the commandment of me, that you love one another just as I loved you.

13. Greater love than this no one has, that someone lay down the life of him for the friends of him.

14. You are friends of me, if you do what I command you.

15. No longer do I call you slaves, because the slave does not know what the lord of him does; but I have called you friends, because all things that I heard from the Father of me I made known to you.

16. You did not choose me, but I chose you and appointed you that you should go and bear fruit and the fruit of you should remain, that whatever you ask the Father in the name of me, he may give [it] to you.

17. These things I command you, that you love one another.

18. If the world hates you, know that me it has hated before you.

19. If from the world you were, the world would love the own; but because from the world you are not, but I chose you from the world, because of this the world hates you.

20. Remember the word that I said to you, 'A slave is not greater than the lord of him.' If me they persecuted, you also they will persecute. If the word of me they kept, the [word] of you also they will keep.

21. But all these things they will do to you because of the name of me, because they do not know the [one] having sent me.

22. If I had not come and spoken to them, sin they would not have; but now excuse they do not have concerning the sin of them.

23. The [one] hating me hates the Father of me also.

24. If the works I had not done among them that no one else did, sin they would not have; but now they have both seen and hated both me and the Father of me.

25. But [this is] that the word may be fulfilled, the [one] written in the law of them, 'They hated me without cause.'

26. When the Advocate comes, whom I will send to you from the Father, the Spirit of the truth, which from the Father proceeds, that [one] will testify concerning me.

27. And you also testify, because from [the] beginning you are with me."

---
## EMET's Textual Notes

1. **v.1 — "I am the true vine":** The seventh and final "I am" statement with predicate. The vine was a symbol of Israel (Psalm 80:8-16, Isaiah 5:1-7, Jeremiah 2:21). Jesus claims to be the true vine — the authentic Israel, the source of fruitfulness that the nation was meant to be but failed to be.

2. **v.2 — "Takes away... prunes":** αἴρει (takes away/lifts up) and καθαίρει (prunes/cleanses). The wordplay links removal and purification. The same Father who removes fruitless branches cleanses fruitful ones.

3. **v.5 — "Apart from me you can do nothing":** χωρὶς ἐμοῦ οὐ δύνασθε ποιεῖν οὐδέν — the double negative (οὐ... οὐδέν) intensifies: "not... nothing" = "absolutely nothing." Complete dependence on the vine.

4. **v.13 — "Greater love has no one than this":** The standard against which all love is measured: laying down one's life for one's friends. Jesus is not stating a general principle — he is describing what he is about to do. The commandment of v.12 ("love as I loved you") finds its definition here.

5. **v.26 — "The Spirit of truth, which from the Father proceeds":** ὃ παρὰ τοῦ πατρὸς ἐκπορεύεται — the filioque clause of the Nicene Creed debates centers partly on this verse. John says the Spirit "proceeds from the Father" — the Western addition "and the Son" (filioque) was a major point of the East-West schism.

— EMET, AI Translator
